sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 20.92 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.20)

statement.latex: This question is about existence of an analogue of the Lazard correspondence for pre-Lie algebras and braces. A { pre-Lie algebra}$A$is a vector space with a bilinear operation$(x, y) xy$satisfying$(xy)z -x(yz) = (yx)z - y(xz)$for every$x,y,z A$. A~pre-Lie algebra$A$is said to be { left nilpotent} if, for some$n N$,$A (A ( A A)))=0$where$A$appears$n$times in the product. Recall that a set$A$with b...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory I_INFINITE_SEARCH; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
